title: Combinatorial State Space of LLM-Based Synthetic Human-Behavior Generation Designs
summary: A typed, machine-readable taxonomy of design choices researchers and practitioners face when generating silicon human-behavior data with LLMs. The `meta` block defines the algebra (variable types, roles, stages, cardinalities, relations, outcome modes); `dimensions` holds the typed tree; `constraints` encodes cross-tree compatibility; `presets` defines named curated subsets for tractable enumeration.

meta.schema_doc: Every group has _meta.cardinality. Every leaf has _meta.var_type and _meta.value_space. Sampling = traverse groups respecting cardinality, draw leaves, then apply `constraints` to filter the cross-product.

stages.design: Choices made before any data are generated.
stages.calibration: Choices made on a held-out alignment subset.
stages.postprocessing: Choices applied to raw model outputs.
stages.evaluation: Choices about how silicon-vs-human comparison is performed.
stages.confirmation: Choices made on a locked confirmation set.
stages.deployment: Choices governing how the artifact is released/used.

variable_roles.design_variable: Manipulated by the researcher across configurations.
variable_roles.outcome_variable: Quantity used to score silicon vs human.
variable_roles.moderator_variable: Hypothesised to change the size/sign of effects.
variable_roles.mediator_variable: On the causal path between design and outcome.
variable_roles.control_variable: Held fixed (or balanced) to isolate other effects.
variable_roles.evaluation_variable: Used only inside the evaluation pipeline.
variable_roles.infrastructure_variable: Platform/runtime choice with no theoretical content.
variable_roles.ethical_variable: Constrained by IRB / governance, not freely sampled.

outcome_modes.regression: Continuous scalar (e.g., MMLU score, mean rating).
outcome_modes.multiclass: K>2 unordered classes (model id, party choice).
outcome_modes.distribution: Outcome is a distribution (PMF over options).
outcome_modes.latent_regression: A latent score (factor / IRT) as outcome.
outcome_modes.embedding: Vector outcome compared via cosine / MMD.
outcome_modes.text_judged: Free text scored by an external LLM judge.
outcome_modes.sequence_model: Sequence outcome modelled with seq2seq / HMM.

analysis_unit_levels.response: A single emitted response, token span, class label, or scalar value.
analysis_unit_levels.item: One survey item, task trial, vignette, stimulus, or decision problem.
analysis_unit_levels.respondent: A stable human or silicon respondent across one or more items.
analysis_unit_levels.session: A contiguous interaction block or experimental session.
analysis_unit_levels.group: A demographic, cultural, treatment, or latent subgroup.
analysis_unit_levels.dataset_task: A single benchmark task derived from one human dataset.
analysis_unit_levels.configuration: One ontology-valid simulation design leaf set.
analysis_unit_levels.model: The concrete LLM endpoint used to instantiate a configuration.
analysis_unit_levels.replicate: A repeated generation seed or stochastic replicate.

feature_encoding_strategies.one_hot: Encode nominal levels with a reference category or full-rank contrasts.
feature_encoding_strategies.ordinal_integer: Encode ordered levels as monotone integers, with categorical sensitivity check.
feature_encoding_strategies.continuous_z: Standardize continuous values using training-set mean and SD only.
feature_encoding_strategies.bounded_continuous_logit: Logit-transform bounded proportions after epsilon clipping.
feature_encoding_strategies.count_log1p: Use log(1+x) for overdispersed counts when used as predictors.
feature_encoding_strategies.compositional_ilr: Use isometric log-ratio coordinates for simplex-valued predictors.
feature_encoding_strategies.distributional_summary: Represent distributions by preregistered distances or moments.
feature_encoding_strategies.embedding_projection: Represent embeddings by frozen projections or distances, not raw high-dimensional leakage features.
feature_encoding_strategies.hierarchical_keys: Retain respondent/item/dataset/configuration keys for grouped splits and clustered errors.

mixed_outcome_resolution_policy.rule: A configuration is analytically valid only when the selected metric family and analysis model structure share at least one legitimate outcome mode with the benchmark task. Hierarchical mixed outcomes preserve both lower-level item/trial information and higher-level respondent/group keys.

relation_types.requires: A presupposes B; selecting A without B is invalid.
relation_types.enables: A makes B legal (where B was otherwise gated).
relation_types.mutually_exclusive: Exactly one of {A,B,...} may be selected.
relation_types.co_recommended: If A then B is strongly suggested (soft).
relation_types.calibrates: B's parameters are tuned by A (procedural link).
relation_types.precedes: A must occur in an earlier stage than B.
relation_types.follows: A must occur in a later stage than B.
relation_types.modulates: A changes the legal value-space of B.
relation_types.overrides: A's selection overrides any B-level choice.

constraint_classes.hard: Configurations violating this are filtered out.
constraint_classes.soft: Configurations get a penalty / warning but remain.

incompatibility_families.REASONING_NEEDS_REASONING_MODEL: High reasoning effort requires a reasoning-class model.
incompatibility_families.INDIVIDUAL_RECOVERY_NEEDS_IDENTITY: Person-level recovery needs stable persona id.
incompatibility_families.FULL_COMPLETION_VS_STOCHASTIC_MISSINGNES: Full-completion gates conflict with stochastic missingness.
incompatibility_families.CROSS_WAVE_NEEDS_PERSISTENCE: Cross-wave consistency needs multi-wave persistent personas.
incompatibility_families.MULTIAGENT_NEEDS_ORCHESTRATION: Multi-agent regimes need an orchestration layer.
incompatibility_families.CRITIC_NEEDS_ITERATION_BUDGET: Actor-critic loops need a non-zero iteration budget.
incompatibility_families.CROSS_CULTURAL_NEEDS_LOCALIZATION: Cross-cultural eval needs localized wording / norms.
incompatibility_families.MULTIMODAL_STIM_NEEDS_MULTIMODAL_MODEL: Image/audio/video stimuli need a multimodal model.

meta.id_path_format: Stable ids = dot-paths of slugified labels; constraints reference these.
meta.presets_doc: Named curated subsets to keep enumeration tractable.

_meta.label: research_problem_formulation
_meta.desc: Why is silicon data being generated and what counts as success?

_meta.label: intended_scientific_function
_meta.desc: Primary epistemic purpose.

_meta.label: descriptive_individual_pointwise
_meta.desc: Reproduce a specific person's specific responses.

_meta.label: descriptive_individual_ranking
_meta.desc: Reproduce within-person ordinal rankings.

_meta.label: descriptive_individual_pattern
_meta.desc: Reproduce within-person response patterns / profiles.

_meta.label: descriptive_marginal_distribution
_meta.desc: Match population-level marginals.

_meta.label: descriptive_joint_distribution
_meta.desc: Match cross-variable joint distributions.

_meta.label: none_unsupervised_simulation
_meta.desc: No human anchor; only internal coherence checks possible.

_meta.label: benchmark_representation_mode
_meta.desc: How a benchmark is treated for analysis. Same dataset can be a score (regression) or a category (multiclass) — different configurations with different downstream eligibility.

_meta.label: aggregate_score_regression
_meta.notes: E.g., total MMLU accuracy as a continuous outcome.

_meta.label: model_or_provider_id_as_class
_meta.notes: Models/providers as discrete classes (e.g., for ablation factor).

_meta.label: hierarchical_mixed_representation
_meta.notes: Item-level classification nested in person-level regression.

_meta.label: model_as_factor_representation
_meta.desc: When the model itself is an experimental factor (for ablation).

_meta.label: model_as_continuous_proxy
_meta.notes: E.g., MMLU score as a stand-in for the model identity.

_meta.label: latent_performance_benchmark
_meta.desc: Optional latent performance representation used when model identity is encoded by external benchmark score.

_meta.label: mmlu_target_49
_meta.notes: Pilot target: choose the OpenRouter-accessible model closest to 49% MMLU in the frozen benchmark table.

_meta.label: mmlu_target_35
_meta.notes: Lowest pilot anchor. MMLU TBD from Liquid AI model card; fallback to mmlu_target_49 if no model with MMLU ≤ 0.43 available.

_meta.label: no_reasoning_cue
_meta.desc: No explicit cognitive instruction; model uses its default response mode.

_meta.label: brief_reflection_cue
_meta.desc: Prompt model to briefly consider the question before answering.

_meta.label: deliberate_carefully_cue
_meta.desc: Instruct deliberative, slow System-2 style reasoning.

_meta.label: think_like_target_person_cue
_meta.desc: Explicitly instruct the model to reason as if it were the target persona.

_meta.label: answer_instinctively_cue
_meta.desc: Suppress deliberation; elicit gut-level System-1 style responding.

_meta.label: use_typical_experience_cue
_meta.desc: Anchor reasoning to typical lived experiences of the target persona.

_meta.label: zero_shot_chain_of_thought_cue
_meta.desc: Append 'Let's think step by step' without exemplars (Wei et al., 2022).

_meta.label: few_shot_chain_of_thought_cue
_meta.desc: Provide worked chain-of-thought exemplars before the target question.

_meta.label: plan_then_respond_cue
_meta.desc: Instruct model to outline a reasoning plan before emitting its final response.

_meta.label: self_consistency_cue
_meta.desc: Generate multiple independent reasoning paths; aggregate final answers by majority vote.

_meta.label: metacognitive_reflection_cue
_meta.desc: Prompt the model to assess its own uncertainty and reasoning quality before finalising response.

_meta.label: perspective_taking_cue
_meta.desc: Explicitly prompt consideration of the item from multiple vantage points before selecting a response.

_meta.label: system_prompt_design
_meta.desc: Content, role, and scope of the system-level prompt passed before any user turn.

_meta.label: no_system_prompt
_meta.desc: No system prompt; model uses its default behavior.

_meta.label: generic_assistant_system_prompt
_meta.desc: Generic helpful-assistant framing with no simulation-specific context.

_meta.label: survey_researcher_role_system_prompt
_meta.desc: Positions the model as a survey research instrument tasked with producing human-like responses.

_meta.label: simulated_human_participant_system_prompt
_meta.desc: System prompt explicitly tells the model it will simulate a human study participant.

_meta.label: persona_constrained_system_prompt
_meta.desc: System prompt carries persona constraints (demographics, traits) that persist across all turns.

_meta.label: task_aware_domain_expert_system_prompt
_meta.desc: System prompt injects domain knowledge relevant to the target task or scale.

_meta.label: confidentiality_and_honesty_system_prompt
_meta.desc: System prompt includes explicit honesty and candor instruction alongside data-use framing.

_meta.label: anti_refusal_guardrail_system_prompt
_meta.desc: System prompt instructs model to comply with all items and suppress refusal behavior.

_meta.label: format_contract_system_prompt
_meta.desc: System prompt specifies output schema, format, and length constraints.

_meta.label: chain_of_thought_scaffold_system_prompt
_meta.desc: System prompt pre-activates step-by-step reasoning mode.

_meta.label: prompt_template_management
_meta.desc: How prompt templates are authored, stored, selected, and parameterised across respondents.

_meta.label: single_monolithic_template
_meta.desc: One static template used for all respondents and items.

_meta.label: parameterised_template_with_slot_filling
_meta.desc: Template contains named slots filled programmatically per respondent.

_meta.label: dynamic_template_assembly
_meta.desc: Template components assembled at runtime by orchestration logic.

_meta.label: template_library_with_selection_policy
_meta.desc: Multiple validated templates; a selection policy rotates or chooses among them.

_meta.label: persona_conditional_template_branching
_meta.desc: Template branch chosen conditionally on persona attributes (e.g., language, cultural background).

_meta.label: llm_generated_bespoke_template
_meta.desc: A meta-LLM call generates the item-specific template before the main generation call.

_meta.label: template_ablation_registry
_meta.desc: Templates versioned in a registry; ablation studies draw from multiple registered versions.

_meta.label: multi_agent_simulation_design
_meta.desc: Optional multi-agent layer.

_meta.label: network_of_peers
_meta.desc: Agents on an arbitrary graph topology.

_meta.label: inter_individual_difference_moderation_recovery
_meta.desc: Recovery of moderating role of individual differences.

_meta.label: hierarchical_mixed_outcome_structure
_meta.desc: Item-level classification nested in person-level regression.

constraints.rationale: High reasoning effort requires a reasoning-class model.
Session memory retention is incompatible with one-shot per-respondent calls.
Joint-association recovery needs non-independent item generation.
Person-level recovery needs identity stability.
Distribution matching needs a non-empty human benchmark.
Full-completion gates contradict stochastic missingness.
Cross-wave dynamics require persistent personas.
Treatment-effect recovery needs an intervention-encoding scheme.
Fairness evaluation requires group labels in the conditioning.
Strict parsers need a structured output contract.
Fine-tuning requires open / fine-tuneable provider class.
Non-trivial agent topologies need an orchestration manager.
Actor-critic loops must declare an iteration control block.
Single-agent runs cannot have a separate critic agent.
Tool-mediated orchestration requires a tool-capable model.
Already mutually exclusive by group cardinality; documented for clarity.
Cross-cultural / new-population tests need geography conditioning.
Counterfactual / treatment effect ambition requires intervention encoding.
Image / video / audio stimuli need a multimodal model.
An unconditioned generic respondent cannot carry persona conditioning.
Distribution metrics co-recommend distribution-recovery targets.
Classification metrics need a classification analysis structure.
Survival metrics need a survival outcome.
Ranking metrics need a ranking outcome.
Text metrics need free-text outputs.
Logit capture is generally only feasible on open / local deployments.
Human-in-the-loop critics imply human oversight governance.
Unbounded iterations require explicit cost control.
Network recovery target needs network outcome structure.
At temperature zero, diversity must come from non-sampling sources.
No-benchmark designs cannot do direct human-silicon comparison.
Correlation metrics require ordered, continuous, ranking, or latent outcomes.
Distribution metrics require distributional or ordered scalar outcome structure.
Prediction-error metrics require scalar, ordinal, classification, or distribution outcome structure.
Calibration metrics require a predicted scalar or class probability structure.
Reliability metrics require repeated scalar, latent, or distributional measurement.
Structural metrics require latent, network, cluster, or association outcome structure.
Hierarchical mixed outcomes must preserve lower-level item or trial information.
Hierarchical mixed outcomes must preserve a higher-level respondent, group, or population unit.
Frontier-tier models (MMLU ≥ 0.88) carry materially higher inference costs; a budget cap is strongly recommended.
High-capability models (MMLU 0.85–0.86) impose elevated per-token costs; budget monitoring is recommended.
High reasoning effort multiplies token usage substantially; at least one budget management leaf must be active.
Chain-of-thought elicitation should be paired with trace capture to enable post-hoc reasoning-quality validation.
A persona-constrained system prompt should be aligned with conditioning variables applied at the user-turn level.

minimal_smoke.desc: Tiny subset for testing the sampler end-to-end.

conventional_core.desc: All conventional=True leaves across the whole tree.

multi_agent_focus.desc: Subtrees relevant to multi-agent / critic-actor design.

evaluation_focus.desc: Evaluation / metric choices plus dependency subtrees needed for valid compatibility checks.
